summary: Iteration 71: Complete testing of Escudo Vecinal (Community Shield) feature. ALL 11 backend API tests passed (GET /stats, GET /incidents, POST /incidents, PATCH /confirm, PATCH /resolve, GET /heatmap). ALL frontend UI tests passed (/escudo-vecinal page loads with map, tab navigation works, report modal opens with all elements, incidents display in sidebar). All 3 route aliases work (/escudo-vecinal, /community-shield, /seguridad-barrio). Homepage navigation has 'Alarmas' and 'Escudo Vecinal' links in header.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/community-shield/stats returns 200 with incident_types, by_type, by_severity
Backend: GET /api/community-shield/incidents returns 200 with incidents array
Backend: POST /api/community-shield/incidents creates new incident successfully
Backend: POST /api/community-shield/incidents with invalid type returns 400
Backend: POST /api/community-shield/incidents works for all 7 types (robo, vandalismo, sospechoso, ruido, emergencia, accidente, otro)
Backend: PATCH /api/community-shield/incidents/{id}/confirm returns 200 or 400 (already confirmed)
Backend: PATCH /api/community-shield/incidents/{id}/confirm returns 404 for non-existent
Backend: PATCH /api/community-shield/incidents/{id}/resolve marks incident as resolved
Backend: PATCH /api/community-shield/incidents/{id}/resolve returns 404 for non-existent
Backend: GET /api/community-shield/heatmap returns points with lat/lng/type/severity
Backend: Integration test - created incident appears in incidents list and heatmap
Frontend: /escudo-vecinal page loads with data-testid='escudo-vecinal-page'
Frontend: 'Escudo Vecinal' title displays correctly
Frontend: Header with ManoProtect logo and 'Reportar' button
Frontend: Tab navigation - 'Mapa en vivo', 'Alertas', 'Como funciona' all work
Frontend: Map container present with Leaflet loaded
Frontend: OpenStreetMap tiles loading correctly
Frontend: 8+ incidents display in sidebar with severity badges
Frontend: Alertas tab shows 13+ incidents in feed view
Frontend: Como funciona tab shows 4 step cards (Observa, Alerta, Confirma, Protege)
Frontend: Report modal opens with type buttons (7), severity buttons (4), title input, description textarea, anonymous checkbox, submit button
Frontend: Report modal closes correctly
Frontend: Homepage header has 'Alarmas' link to /alarmas-hogar
Frontend: Homepage header has 'Escudo Vecinal' link to /escudo-vecinal
Frontend: /escudo-vecinal route works
Frontend: /community-shield route works (same page)
Frontend: /seguridad-barrio route works (same page)

test_credentials.note: No auth required for most endpoints

seed_data_creation: Test incidents created during testing with prefix TEST_ (e.g., TEST_integration_*, TEST_resolve_test_*, TEST_confirm_test)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 71 tested the new Escudo Vecinal (Community Shield) feature comprehensively. Backend: All 6 API endpoints working correctly - stats returns incident_types with icons/colors/labels, incidents list returns active incidents within 24h, POST creates incidents with validation, PATCH /confirm increments confirmations (status changes to 'confirmed' at 3+), PATCH /resolve marks as resolved, heatmap returns points for map display. Frontend: EscudoVecinal.jsx renders correctly with Leaflet map centered on Valencia (39.4699, -0.3763), tab navigation switches content, report modal has all form elements, incidents appear in sidebar and feed. All 3 routes work (/escudo-vecinal, /community-shield, /seguridad-barrio). Homepage nav links verified.

returns: points with latitude, longitude, type, severity

components.header: ManoProtect logo + Alarmas + Productos + Reportar button
components.hero: EXCLUSIVO MANOPROTECT badge, Escudo Vecinal title, stats (Protectores, Alertas semana, Alertas mes, 24/7)
components.map: Leaflet with OpenStreetMap tiles, centered on Valencia (39.4699, -0.3763)
components.sidebar: Ultimas alertas list with incident cards
components.feed: Feed de alertas vecinales with confirm buttons
components.info: 4 step cards (1. Observa, 2. Alerta, 3. Confirma, 4. Protege)
components.modal: ReportModal with type buttons, severity buttons, title, description, anonymous checkbox

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are real, connected to MongoDB
